description: Full merged scope for the entire Ares CRM master implementation plan: Phase 0 baseline, Phase 1 lead wedge, shared autonomy foundations, Phase 2 planner, Phase 3 bounded executor, Phase 4 workflow agent, and Phase 5 guarded autonomous operator. This replaces the phase-1-only PRD.
verificationCommands: uv run python -m compileall app tests

stories.title: Lock the baseline runtime and verify readiness
stories.description: Confirm the current runtime surface is stable enough to host the Ares autonomy stack before changing anything.
acceptanceCriteria: The runtime boots cleanly on the current branch.
The current command, approval, run, and replay surface is still intact.
Mission Control still renders the lead-machine shell and read models.
The marketing lane still works as a separate execution path.
The WAT architecture doc remains the operating-model reference.
The baseline still uses the in-memory control plane where it already does.
verificationCommands: uv run pytest tests/api/test_commands.py tests/api/test_runs.py tests/api/test_replays.py tests/api/test_mission_control_phase3.py tests/domains/marketing/test_marketing_flow.py -q
uv run python -m compileall app tests

stories.title: Lock the Ares lead models and county/source contracts
stories.description: Create the Ares domain models and tests that define the five counties, source lanes, run request defaults, and explicit estate_of detection on lead records.
acceptanceCriteria: AresCounty enumerates Harris, Tarrant, Montgomery, Dallas, and Travis in that order.
AresRunRequest coerces county strings into enums and defaults include_briefs and include_drafts to true.
AresLeadRecord exposes estate_of as true when the owner name or tax-only source indicates an estate-of property.
Package layout tests pass for the new domain module.

stories.title: Implement probate-first matching, tax overlays, and tiering
stories.description: Build the matching and scoring service that treats probate as the primary lane, applies tax delinquency as an overlay, and gives verified probate plus tax-delinquent leads the highest rank.
acceptanceCriteria: Probate is the primary source lane.
Tax delinquency is treated as an overlay filter on probate records.
Overlap between probate and verified tax delinquency ranks highest.
Tax-only work is limited to estate-of properties and then confirmed for true delinquency.
Service tests cover the matching and tiering rules.

stories.title: Add lead briefs and outreach drafts
stories.description: Generate operator-facing lead briefs and human-review-only outreach drafts for the ranked opportunities without auto-sending anything.
acceptanceCriteria: Ares can generate a concise lead brief for each ranked opportunity.
Ares can generate outreach drafts that remain pending human approval.
Draft output preserves the lead rationale, county, source lane, and rank.
Copy service tests cover the brief and draft generation paths.

stories.title: Add the Ares API route and wire it into the app
stories.description: Expose the Ares runtime through the API surface and wire the route into the FastAPI app so the phase-1 slice can be called end to end.
acceptanceCriteria: The Ares route is registered in the app.
The route can execute the phase-1 runtime path.
API tests cover the expected request and response shape.
The app still boots with the new route wired in.

stories.title: Update repo docs and memory with the full master scope
stories.description: Update the repo-facing docs and memory surfaces so the branch of record reflects the full merged Ares phased implementation plan, not just the first wedge.
acceptanceCriteria: The repo docs point to the full merged Ares phased implementation plan as the source of truth.
The five counties remain explicitly named in the handoff docs.
The probate-first plus tax-overlay rule is stated plainly.
Human approval remains required before sending drafts in Phase 1.
The PRD remains the handoff artifact for overnight loop work.

stories.title: Build the versioned agent registry foundation
stories.description: Create the agent registry primitives so Ares can track versioned agents instead of one mutable blob.
acceptanceCriteria: Each agent record has a name, purpose, revision, allowed tools, risk policy, output contract, and active revision.
The registry supports versioned agents rather than a single mutable configuration blob.
Registry tests prove the model and service round-trip cleanly.

stories.title: Build the durable memory foundation
stories.description: Create state persistence that survives past a single run and captures the operator and lead history that later phases need.
acceptanceCriteria: Durable memory can store market preferences and county defaults.
Durable memory can store lead history, outreach history, operator decisions, outcomes, and exceptions.
Memory tests prove the state survives across loads and saves.

stories.title: Build the deterministic tool policy foundation
stories.description: Create the deterministic, auditable tool policy layer with explicit allowlists and hard approvals for risky calls.
acceptanceCriteria: Tool calls use explicit allowlists.
Inputs and outputs are typed.
The policy blocks magical side effects.
Risky calls require hard approval.
The policy service exposes an audit trail and kill-switch behavior.

stories.title: Build the evaluation-loop foundation
stories.description: Create the evaluation loop primitives so Ares can measure whether autonomy is actually improving anything.
acceptanceCriteria: The evaluation loop measures lead quality, response quality, conversion quality, false positives, duplicate work, and operator corrections.
Evaluation results are durable and inspectable.
Evaluation tests prove the metrics contract is stable.

stories.title: Build Mission Control visibility for autonomy
stories.description: Expose the operator-visible read models needed to trust the system while the later phases come online.
acceptanceCriteria: Mission Control shows the current phase, active run, pending approvals, and failed steps.
Mission Control surfaces lead quality, confidence, and the next action the agent is about to take.
Visibility tests cover the operator-facing read models.

stories.title: Define planner models and contracts
stories.description: Create the planning models that let Ares turn a goal into an explicit, structured execution plan.
acceptanceCriteria: The planner models accept a goal, source lanes, checks, and step-by-step plans.
The planner models can represent why a plan makes sense.
The planner models require approval before side-effecting action.
Planner model tests and package layout checks pass.

stories.title: Implement planner service logic
stories.description: Teach Ares how to choose source lanes, decide which checks to run, produce a step-by-step plan, and explain the plan to the operator.
acceptanceCriteria: The planner accepts goals like finding probate plus tax-delinquent leads in a county slice.
The planner chooses source lanes and checks that match the goal.
The planner produces a concrete step-by-step plan and explains why it makes sense.
The planner asks for approval before any side-effecting action.

stories.title: Expose planner output through the API and Mission Control
stories.description: Add the planner API route and wire planner output into Mission Control so operators can approve or reject plans in one glance.
acceptanceCriteria: The planner API route is registered and reachable.
Mission Control can surface planner output for review.
API tests cover the planner request and response shape.
The app still boots with the planner route wired in.

stories.title: Define execution contracts and guardrails
stories.description: Create the execution models and enforce the narrow limits that keep Ares bounded while it starts to act on its own.
acceptanceCriteria: The execution models represent narrow, bounded lead-generation runs.
Action budgets, retry limits, county or market scope limits, approved tool allowlists, audit trail, and kill-switch behavior are defined.
Execution and policy tests cover the guardrail contract.

stories.title: Implement the bounded execution service
stories.description: Teach Ares how to fetch county data, normalize and dedupe records, enrich them, run overlay matching, produce ranked outputs, generate drafts and task suggestions, and queue follow-up work.
acceptanceCriteria: The execution service can fetch county data and normalize it.
The execution service dedupes overlap records and enriches available data.
The execution service runs overlay matching and returns ranked lead outputs.
The execution service can generate drafts, task suggestions, and follow-up work.
Failures are visible, recoverable, and interruptible.

stories.title: Expose bounded execution through the API and Mission Control
stories.description: Wire the bounded execution path into the Ares API and Mission Control so the operator surface can launch and inspect narrow runs.
acceptanceCriteria: The Ares API route can trigger the bounded execution path.
Mission Control can show execution results and run state.
The app boots with the bounded-execution wiring in place.
verificationCommands: uv run pytest tests/api/test_ares_runtime.py tests/api/test_mission_control_phase3.py tests/test_package_layout.py -q

stories.title: Define workflow and state models
stories.description: Create the workflow models that let Ares own a narrow playbook from intake to next-best-action.
acceptanceCriteria: Workflow models can represent a county or market slice, state across steps, and the next-best-action.
Workflow models can record what already happened without losing history.
Workflow model tests cover the new contract.

stories.title: Implement playbook, state, and evaluation services
stories.description: Build the playbook runner, state tracker, and workflow evaluation services that let Ares repeat a narrow workflow with low operator overhead.
acceptanceCriteria: Ares can own a workflow from intake to next-best-action.

The playbook service can choose the county or market slice, pull probate and tax signals, enrich and score leads, generate outreach, create follow-up tasks, monitor responses, and suggest next actions.
The state service remembers what already happened and handles retries and fallbacks.
The eval service surfaces exceptions instead of silently dropping them.

stories.title: Integrate the workflow agent into execution and Mission Control
stories.description: Wire the workflow agent into the execution service and operator surface so the repeated county workflow becomes a visible, testable capability.
acceptanceCriteria: Human approval is still required before send, contract, and disposition actions.
Policy checks run on every high-risk step.
Drift detection and evaluation after each run are in place.
Mission Control can explain the major decisions and failures.

stories.title: Wire the guarded autonomous operator into Mission Control and app startup
stories.description: Hook the agent registry, durable memory, evaluation loop, and operator surface together so Ares can run approved objectives with minimal supervision inside explicit boundaries.
acceptanceCriteria: Ares can pick up approved objectives, execute playbooks, monitor results, adapt within bounds, escalate only when needed, and keep a full record of what it did and why.
No free-form spending, unbounded outreach, contract signing without policy gates, silent escalations, or cross-market sprawl are allowed.
Mission Control and app startup expose the guarded operator surface.
Operators trust the logs, decisions, and exceptions.
